title: globba brachyanthera var. rubra
intro: Globba brachyanthera var. rubra, also known as the red globba or red kaempferia, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have various health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain. The plant is used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, to treat a range of ailments. The health benefits of Globba brachyanthera var. rubra are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
